about.intro: A developer who always thinks about **UX** from the *user's perspective* to create convenient and intuitive interfaces.
about.description: With a *frontend development-focused* mindset, I design user flows and screen structures, using React and Next.js to build **intuitive interfaces** and ***maintainable components***.
about.full:
I am a junior developer who has grown by building and improving my own projects.
I enjoy the process of turning ideas into working products.
I also like exploring new technologies, and I believe the best way to learn something is to use it directly.

Through React and JavaScript-based web projects, I have experienced the overall frontend development process, including UI implementation, REST API integration, state management, and deployment.
In team projects, I was responsible for frontend development and GitHub collaboration, and I focused on building interfaces that users can easily understand and reusable structures that are easy to maintain.

When building projects, I do not think it is enough for something to simply work.
I try to write code that is clean, understandable, and easy to maintain.
When I encounter a problem, I prefer to understand how it actually works rather than quickly moving on.

My current goal is to experience larger projects with real impact and further develop my skills, especially in frontend development and system design.
I want to become a developer who continues to grow by understanding how technologies work and solving problems, rather than simply applying new technologies.

projects.intro:
Introducing **projects** completed from 
 *planning to implementation* 
 with a focus on *UX*.

items.title: Scholar Mate
items.description:
ScholarMate is a web service that brings scholarship information scattered across multiple organizations into one searchable place, recommends scholarships based on a user's academic status, region, income level, and grades, and helps users manage deadlines for scholarships they are interested in. 

 I was responsible for frontend development on this project, implementing scholarship search, personalized recommendations, favorites, calendar, community, messaging, and authentication flows. 
 Beyond building screens, I handled API integration, server-state management, optimistic updates, performance optimization, and accessibility checks, connecting the full flow from discovering scholarships to managing application schedules.

items.title: MoodWave
items.description:
MoodWave is a web service centered on Spotify music discovery and playback. It recommends songs based on the user's mood and current weather, analyzes their most-listened-to genres, and suggests popular tracks from those genres. 

 I implemented the shared layout, hash-based routing, home, search, playlist, and mood recommendation interfaces, along with Spotify OAuth authentication and a Web Playback SDK-based player. After the project, I improved page lifecycle management, request cancellation, data caching, external SDK lazy loading, and image optimization.

items.title: DevTube
items.description:
DevTube is a learning video collection web service that uses the YouTube API to help developers and certification learners discover and watch relevant educational videos.

Built with React and Vite, it includes recommended videos, recommended developer channels, course categories, search, channel detail pages, and video detail pages.
The responsive layout was improved for mobile usability, including video playback and sidebar behavior, while React Query was introduced to handle API caching, request deduplication, and structured error states.

The project also improves user experience and code quality through skeleton UI, error message, image lazy loading, font optimization, SEO meta handling, accessibility attributes, and ESLint/Prettier configuration.
All changes were verified through production builds, lint checks, and format checks.

items.title: Feelog
items.description:
Feelog is an emotion journaling web service where users can record their daily feelings, revisit past entries through search and emotion filters, and add personal reflections.

Using Cursor AI, I broke requirements into functional tasks and designed prompts to implement diary CRUD, search, filtering, pagination, reflections, a photo archive, and authentication flows. 
 I reviewed the generated code, reproduced and resolved issues, improved React Query state management, Next.js API Routes, Supabase integration, user authorization, and RLS security, and validated the results through production builds and E2E tests.

roadmap.description: A roadmap where I share the experiences I've gained throughout my software journey and the technologies I've learned.

items.description: I entered university and was first introduced to programming through the Software Convergence major. Through major courses, I learned Python and C, building a foundation in programmingand object-oriented thinking.

After completing my military service, I studied HTML, CSS, and JavaScriptthrough a web programming course and began building static and dynamic websites. While working on projects, I also learned how to connect and use databases. In addition, 
 I studied Java in an object-oriented programming course, and built foundational computer science knowledge through major coursessuch as data structures, algorithms, computer architecture, and discrete mathematics.

In the first semester, I studied Android Studio and Kotlinthrough a mobile programming course. 
 Based on that experience,I developed an Android application using Raspberry Piin a convergence software project during the second semester. 
 I also learned MySQL through a database course and built foundational knowledgeacross computer science through major courses such as computer networks, systems analysis and design, operating systems, and software engineering.

While working as the frontend developer in sophomore and junior-year projects, I became interested in designing and implementing user interfaces, which led me to take charge of frontend development for my graduation project. 
 After that, I clarified my career direction as a frontend developerand focused on learning technologies such as React, JavaScript, and Tailwind CSS. 
 I applied what I learned to my graduation project and ultimately won an Excellence Awardin the ESG category at my university's Capstone Design Competition.

After graduating from university, I joined the [LG U+] URECA SW training program, a frontend developer bootcamp, where I am strengthening my frontend skillswith a focus on React and JavaScript. 
 Through personal and team projects, I have also gained experience with technologies such as TypeScript, Next.js, Figma, and Spring Boot. 
 By using AI to quickly learn unfamiliar technologies and apply them to projects, I am also improving my ability to utilize AI and strengthening my collaboration skills.

contact.description:
I am open to new projects and collaboration opportunities.
I would like to create better user experiences together.
